item.title: Android Artifacts
item.description:
Android phones do not immediately upgrades but rollouts tend to be taken up slowly (months to years).

Google is developer but manufacturers and carriers also have a role in upgrade process

Android devices now have full device encryption
item.dc:creator: Daniel Haggerty

item.title: iPhone Artifacts
item.description:
1. Acquisition
  2. Apple Device Identifiers
  3. iTunes Backups, info.plist
  4. iMessage and SMS/MMS Messages
  5. Call logs
  6. Contacts
  7. Calendars and Reminders
  8. Safari
  9. Third-party app data
  10. Anatomy of an Application

1. Acquisition

  • Property lockdown pairing plist will be located at C:\ProgramData\Apple\Lockdown
  • Can copy plist from target comp to examiner.
  • May …
item.dc:creator: Daniel Haggerty

item.title: Docker Certified Associate
item.description:
Docker

docker run dans-container

Is equivalent too

docker create dans-container
docker start dans-container

If:

docker run busybox ping google.com

dockerfile

FROM node:alpine

WORKDIR '/app'

COPY package.json .

RUN npm install

COPY . .

CMD ["npm", "start"]

docker-compose.yml

version: '3'
services:
  redis-server:
    image: 'redis'
  node-app:
    restart: always
    build: .
    ports:
      - "4001 …
item.dc:creator: Daniel Haggerty

item.title: Docker Commands
item.description:
Docker

docker run dans-container

Is equivalent too

docker create dans-container
docker start dans-container

If:

docker run busybox ping google.com

To view that container still running:

docker ps
docker ps --all

To stop container: docker stop tries to cleanup, docker kill is more brutal.

docker stop containerid
docker kill containerid …
item.dc:creator: Daniel Haggerty

item.title: Linux Privilege Escalation
item.description:
privesc_linux
UPGRADE SHELL python -c 'import pty; pty.spawn("bin/bash")'
ctrl-Z
stty raw -echo
fg

Enumerate and run this:

which awk perl python ruby gcc cc vi vim nmap find netcat nc wget tftp ftp 2>/dev/null

If you don’t see a compiler such as GCC …
item.dc:creator: Daniel Haggerty

item.title: Windows Privilege Escalation
item.description:
icacls scsiaccess.exe
...
Everyone: (I)(F)

crosscompile useradd.c i686-w64-mingw32-gcc -o scsiaccess.exe useradd.c wce -w fgdump.exe type 127.0.0.1.pwdump john --wordlist=/usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt 127.0.0.1.pwdump Is there python on dev windows machine Use pyinstaller on dev windows machine …
item.dc:creator: Daniel Haggerty

item.title: Linux Buffer Overflow
item.description:
Linux Buffer Overflow (Crossfire)

Download a version of the vulnerable app Crossfire

Setup safe environment: iptables only allow traffic from loopback interface=targetip

iptables –A, -p, -d, -j # Append, protocol, destination, jump crossfire listens on port 13327 iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --destination-port 13327 ! -d 127.0.0.1 -j …
item.dc:creator: Daniel Haggerty

item.title: Windows Buffer Overflow
item.description:
Windows Buffer Overflow (SLMail)

Seattle Lab Mail (SLMail) is fantastic vulnerable app to practise your buffer overflow on. You will have to download a version of the vulnerable SLMail to a Windows VM. The SLMail buffer overflow affects the POP3 PASS command. Both the app itself and the exploit are …
item.dc:creator: Daniel Haggerty

item.title: "OSCP: Notes for exam"
item.description:
Everyone has one. This is mine. A collection of OSCP prep-notes from various other guides and sources over the internet as well as my own experience within the Hack The Box labs and in the Penetration Testing with Kali Linux labs.

Nmap (host/port)

# nmap all ports
nmap -p- -oA …
item.dc:creator: Daniel Haggerty

item.title: "FTK: Imaging a Windows PC"
item.description: Drive acquisition is one the first steps a Forensic Analyst will have to carry out at the beginning of case. These acquistions are often carried out remotely by VPN or by the the client (or client IT department) where the computer is located. Acquiring such images from a drive has …
item.dc:creator: Daniel Haggerty

item.title: Network+ - Cabling & Topology
item.description:
Topology

A network topology is defined by the lines and nodes that make up the network. This can be further delineated into two twpes of topology: physical vs logical topology.

Physical topology is the geometric pattern of the connected nodes of a network, whereas logical topology defines the pattern of …
item.dc:creator: Daniel Haggerty

item.title: Passing the OSCP Exam - Trying Smarter
item.description:
The definition of insanity is doing the same thing over and over again, but expecting different results.

Albert Einstein

If, like me, you have no hacking experience and no computer science background you have probably spent time in the Penetration Testing With Kali Linux (PWK) labs proving Einstein's theory of …
item.dc:creator: Daniel Haggerty

item.title: Network+ - Network Models
item.description:
Network Models

There are two main models that represent networks. The OSI and TCP/IP Model.

OSI Model

  1. Physical (Cables)
  2. Data Link (Network cards, Switches, components with MAC Addresses)
  3. Network (Routers, Logical Addresses, IP Addresses)
  4. Transport (Assembly/Disassembly layer for packets)
  5. Session (Connection between two systems)
  6. Presentation (Converts data into …
item.dc:creator: Daniel Haggerty
